description:
You are a Linux systems engineer performing security hardening.  
A colleague has exported the list of running services and their desired hardening state to the file  

  /home/user/security/services.csv  

The CSV file already exists.  
Format of /home/user/security/services.csv (including the header row):  
service,status  
…one service per line…  

Only two status values are ever present:  
• enable  
• disable  

Your job is to quickly identify every service that must be disabled and hand the list to an automation framework that expects a very specific JSON file.

Required end-state (what the automated checker will verify):

1. Create a new file  
   /home/user/security/services_to_disable.json  

   a. The file must contain a single JSON array (no surrounding object) listing **only** the service names whose status is “disable”.  
   b. The array must be sorted in strict ASCII/lexicographical order (i.e. standard sort with LC_ALL=C).  
   c. The array must contain no duplicates.  
   d. The file must end with a single trailing newline.  
   Example of the required structure (the exact elements will differ):  
   ["avahi-daemon","bluetooth","cups"]\n  

   NOTES:  
   • No spaces are allowed after commas or before the newline.  
   • The array must be on exactly one line.  

2. Append a one-line log entry to  
   /home/user/security/hardening.log  

   • The log entry must be appended (create the file if it does not exist).  
   • The line must read exactly (without quotes):  
     services_to_disable.json created  
   • The line must be terminated with a single newline character.  
   • Do not write anything else to this log file.  

Constraints & tips:

• Accomplish the task using a single terminal command or a command pipeline executed from the shell.  
• Do not open an interactive editor.  
• Assume you are already located in /home/user or can reference absolute paths.  
• You do NOT have root privileges; all work must occur inside /home/user.  

When you are finished, both /home/user/security/services_to_disable.json and /home/user/security/hardening.log must exist and match the exact formats above. An automated test will check byte-for-byte compliance, including newlines and ordering. No additional files should be modified or created.
truth:
Initial file present before the agent starts:

1. Path: /home/user/security/services.csv  
   Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)  
   Contents (including final newline):  
   service,status  
   cups,disable  
   ssh,enable  
   avahi-daemon,disable  
   bluetooth,disable  
   ntp,enable  
   (file ends with newline)

Expected files after successful completion:

1. /home/user/security/services_to_disable.json  
   Permissions: 0644  
   Exact contents (byte-for-byte, including the trailing newline):  
   ["avahi-daemon","bluetooth","cups"]\n

2. /home/user/security/hardening.log  
   Permissions: 0644  
   Exact contents:  
   services_to_disable.json created\n
